name: Creole Style Tomato Soup with Goat's Cheese Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 8 cups chopped fresh tomatoes (peeled and seeded)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the fryer.
2. In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
3. Add the onions, celery, and carrots to the pan. Season with salt, pepper, and cayenne. Sauté for 4-5 minutes until slightly softened.
4. Stir in the garlic and tomatoes. Cook for an additional 3-4 minutes, stirring often.
5. Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
6. Using a hand-held blender, puree half of the soup.
7. Stir in the parsley and re-season with salt and pepper if needed.
8. Add the heavy cream and stir until well combined.
9. For the dumplings:
a. In a small mixing bowl, combine the Goat's cheese, extra-virgin olive oil, and basil. Mix until fully incorporated.
b. Season with salt and pepper.
c. Place 1 tablespoon of the cheese mixture in the center of each wonton wrapper.
d. Brush the edges of the wrappers with a little water.
e. Bring two corners of the wrappers together and seal, forming a triangle.
f. Fry the wontons for 1-2 minutes or until golden and crispy. Drain on a paper-lined plate.
10. Serve the soup in shallow bowls garnished with the dumplings and finely chopped parsley.
Yield: 10 cups of soup, 16 dumplings
